mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top quartile of PAYEMS growth months fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, forward guidance anchors short-term rates near zero, reducing the equity discount rate and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signal robust consumer demand and labor market health, amplifying corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term RSAFS times UNRATE yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in predictive regressions over any 3-year window, or the portfolio sorted on the interaction underperforms the single-signal benchmark by more than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns because rising capacity utilization lifts corporate margins without triggering aggressive monetary tightening when inflation (CPIAUCSL) is stable.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year is between 1% and 3%, INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.1 million units signal durable construction demand and upstream manufacturing orders, leading to broad economic momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where HOUST exceeds 1100, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates accommodative monetary policy is successfully transmitting to real activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with real sector response
failure_condition: The joint condition of low FEDFUNDS and rising INDPRO fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that compresses corporate earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) expansion reflects aggregate nominal demand deflated by price stability; persistent growth in real output drives corporate revenue and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to show positive correlation with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns with t-statistic above 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.
